class.name: AsioAudioDevice
class.brief_description: Full-duplex ASIO device — input and output on one driver (Windows only).
class.description: [AsioAudioDevice] opens a single ASIO driver with both input and output buffers. This is the correct way to use one interface (e.g. a Focusrite) for capture and playback at the same time, since ASIO allows only one open driver per process. It can route input straight to the output on the ASIO callback thread for true low-latency monitoring (see [method set_direct_monitor]), emit captured input blocks for analysis via [signal audio_frame], and mix game audio into the output via [method push_frames]. Typical guitar-rig usage: [method open] the interface, [method set_direct_monitor] to [code]true[/code] so the input is heard through the output, then [method start]. Connect [signal audio_frame] if you also want to run pitch/chord detection on the input. [b]Platform support:[/b] Windows only. On other platforms the GDExtension is not loaded and the class does not exist; guard usage with [code]ClassDB.class_exists("AsioAudioDevice")[/code]. [b]Related classes:[/b] Use [AsioAudioInput] or [AsioAudioOutput] when you only need one direction. Do not open the same interface with those classes while an [AsioAudioDevice] holds it — ASIO permits only one open driver per process. [codeblock] var dev := AsioAudioDevice.new() add_child(dev) if dev.open("Focusrite USB ASIO", 0): dev.set_direct_monitor(true) dev.set_monitor_gain(1.0) dev.audio_frame.connect(_on_audio_frame) # optional, for detection dev.start() [/codeblock]

method.name: close
method.description: Stops the stream (if running), disposes the ASIO buffers and releases the driver. Safe to call multiple times; called automatically when the node is freed.

method.name: get_buffer_size
method.description: Returns the buffer size, in samples per channel, the driver was opened with. Valid after a successful [method open].

method.name: get_driver_list
method.description: Returns the names of all installed ASIO drivers. Pass one of these to [method open]. Empty if none are installed.

method.name: get_frames_free
method.description: Returns how many stereo frames can currently be queued with [method push_frames] before the game-audio ring buffer is full.

method.name: get_input_channels
method.description: Returns the number of input channels on the opened driver. [signal audio_frame] is emitted once per channel.

method.name: get_min_buffer_size
method.description: Returns the minimum buffer size, in samples, the opened driver supports. Valid after a successful [method open].

method.name: get_monitor_gain
method.description: Returns the current direct-monitor gain (see [method set_monitor_gain]).

method.name: get_output_channels
method.description: Returns the number of output channels on the opened driver. Channel 0 carries the left signal and channel 1 the right; further channels are silent.

method.name: get_sample_rate
method.description: Returns the sample rate, in Hz, the driver is running at. Match any audio pushed with [method push_frames] to this rate.

method.name: is_direct_monitor
method.description: Returns whether direct input-to-output monitoring is enabled (see [method set_direct_monitor]).

method.name: is_mono_input
method.description: Returns whether the monitored input is folded to mono (see [method set_mono_input]).

method.name: is_running
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] while the stream is running (between [method start] and [method stop]).

method.name: open
method.description: Loads the named ASIO driver (from [method get_driver_list]) and creates both input and output buffers. Returns [code]true[/code] on success. [param buffer_size]: [code]0[/code] = driver preferred (default), [code]-1[/code] = minimum (lowest monitoring latency), any positive value is clamped to the supported range. Call [method start] afterwards. Calling [method open] again first closes any previously opened driver.

method.name: push_frames
method.description: Mixes stereo game audio into the output ([code]x[/code] = left, [code]y[/code] = right), summed with the direct monitor if enabled. Returns the number of frames accepted (fewer than requested if the ring buffer is full; check [method get_frames_free]). Call from the main thread.

method.name: set_direct_monitor
method.description: Enables or disables routing the input straight to the output on the ASIO callback thread. This gives the lowest possible monitoring latency (no main-thread hop). Input channel 0/1 map to output channel 0/1; a mono input is duplicated to both.

method.name: set_mono_input
method.description: When enabled, sums the input channels into one mono signal sent to both output channels. Use this when the instrument is plugged into a single input, so it isn't heard in only one ear. Affects the direct monitor only.

method.name: set_monitor_gain
method.description: Sets the linear gain applied to the direct monitor signal ([code]1.0[/code] = unity). Has no effect unless [method set_direct_monitor] is enabled.

method.name: show_control_panel
method.description: Opens the driver's own settings panel, if it provides one. Requires a driver to be opened first.

method.name: start
method.description: Starts the full-duplex stream. Must be called after a successful [method open]. Returns [code]true[/code] on success.

method.name: stop
method.description: Stops the stream. The driver stays open and can be restarted with [method start]. Does nothing if not running.

signal.name: audio_frame
signal.description: Emitted once per input channel for every captured block, with [param data] as normalized float samples and [param channel] the source channel (0-based). Emitted on the main thread (deferred), so it is safe to use from GDScript. This is independent of direct monitoring — you can analyse the input while it is monitored to the output.

signal.name: reset_requested
signal.description: Emitted when the driver requests a reset (e.g. after a control-panel change). Respond by calling [method close] then [method open]/[method start] again.

signal.name: sample_rate_changed
signal.description: Emitted when the driver reports a sample rate change. [param new_rate] is the new rate in Hz.
